creator: bryan@shatow.net
text:
Defining SINGLE options forces you to pick 1 and only 1. Setting the SINGLE in the OPTIONS_DEFINE lets you do 0 or 1, but then forces you to select that as a "feature". It should still be possible to select *0* with SINGLE without adding it to OPTIONS_DEFINE.

How-To-Repeat: 	Using this, it should pass if selecting none or selecting 1. It should fail if selecting both. Before my patch, it fails if selecting 0.

	OPTIONS_SINGLE= DB_BACKEND
	OPTIONS_SINGLE_DB_BACKEND=      BDB BDB1
	OPTIONS_DEFAULT=        BDB

	--

	Using this, it should pass if selecting none or selecting 1. It should fail if selecting both. Before and after my patch, it passes if selecting 0.

	OPTIONS_DEFINE=	DB_BACKEND
	OPTIONS_SINGLE= DB_BACKEND
	OPTIONS_SINGLE_DB_BACKEND=      BDB BDB1
	OPTIONS_DEFAULT=        BDB
